current_cloud_blocker: The stable confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask lead is still materially worse than baseline on the depth terms (val_loss_conf_depth 0.1181 -> 0.2288 and val_loss_reg_depth 0.1181 -> 0.1759). The tested Camera_B1 quality-conditioned family now fails both as conf-target normalization and as direct loss-routing attempts: the exact linear qmin->qmax whole-foreground scale0.5 rule regressed val_loss_conf_depth from 0.2289 to 0.2776 and val_loss_reg_depth from 0.1760 to 0.1848; the softer whole-foreground scale0.75 fallback regressed them from 0.2289 to 0.2483 and 0.1760 to 0.1797; the whole-foreground quadratic scale0.5 candidate regressed them from 0.2288 to 0.2587 and 0.1759 to 0.1817; the foreground-interior linear scale0.75 candidate still regressed them from 0.2288 to 0.2432 and 0.1759 to 0.1787 while keeping camera/T flat; the whole-foreground confmask-drop candidate regressed them from 0.2289 to 0.3018 and 0.1760 to 0.1959; the whole-foreground joint depth-loss q>=2.75 scale0.75 candidate regressed them from 0.2288 to 0.2546 and 0.1759 to 0.1805; the qlinear whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.75 candidate regressed them from 0.2288 to 0.2458 and 0.1759 to 0.1790; the qquadratic whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.75 candidate regressed them from 0.2288 to 0.2417 and 0.1759 to 0.1783; the qquadratic whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.875 frontier candidate still regressed them from 0.2288 to 0.2344 and 0.1759 to 0.1770 while keeping camera/T flat; the first whole-foreground decoupled conf/reg routing follow-up regressed them to 0.2378 and 0.1776 while keeping camera/T flat; the non-wholefg foreground-edge qquadratic joint-depth frontier tightened that boundary to 0.2330/0.1767, 0.2304/0.1762, and 0.2293/0.1760 at scales 0.5/0.75/0.875 without crossing promotion; the foreground-edge decoupled follow-up still landed at 0.2302/0.1762; and adding a hard gt depth_conf<=p60 threshold inside that promising edge route regressed to 0.2411/0.1837 despite improving camera to 0.0184. The blocker is therefore no longer missing plumbing and no longer a hidden whole-frame masking bug; supervised point masks are already foreground-clipped, the current rawpool line already concentrates depth supervision on the anchor-supervised view under zju_min_supervised_views=1, and the remaining headroom appears to require a softer non-wholefg selectivity change rather than another whole-foreground scalar, another whole-foreground decoupled near-neighbor, another plain edge-band scalar, or another hard pixel threshold. The stable current local lead therefore remains confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask and cloud must remain closed.
question: After the whole-foreground normalization, whole-foreground routing, and first non-wholefg edge-band routing frontiers all failed to beat the stable lead, and a hard gt depth_conf threshold inside the promising edge-band route also failed, is any softer non-wholefg selectivity-changing quality-conditioned depth-loss question still worth approving without harming camera/T?
why_now: The post-v9 residual cluster remains locally bounded.

The full Camera_B1-conditioned follow-up family has already been exercised locally: the whole-frame variant failed the 10-train / 5-val gate, and the narrower bottom20 variant failed the 100-train / 20-val long gate after only a short-gate marginal win.

The quality-signal audit did define a new bounded question and the first local candidate was executed end-to-end, but that Camera_B1 + quality>=3.0 + foreground-bottom20 + scale 0.5 variant only nudged conf_depth while leaving reg_depth flat.

The original postmortem on the first 512 train samples shows the hard q>=3.0 gate is over-selective: it excludes 4 / 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows and touches only 0.7476% of conf-depth-supervised pixels.

A follow-up quality-region boundary check on a fresh 512-sample train slice shows bottom20 is too narrow as well: all 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows still have positive non-bottom conf_depth deltas, while q>=3.0 + whole foreground covers 22.6975% of conf-depth-supervised pixels versus only 0.7983% for q>=3.0 + bottom20.

A follow-up rule-shape sweep then shows the remaining broader hard-threshold whole-foreground alternatives are still not the right next question: q>=3.0 whole foreground still omits 4 / 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows, q>=2.75 still omits 1 / 10, while a continuous qmin->qmax whole-foreground reference rule covers all 10 bad rows with a 5.1155% effective reduction fraction on the same fresh 512-sample train slice.

The continuous quality-to-scale knob now exists locally and the first exact linear qmin->qmax + whole-foreground + scale0.5 candidate was executed end-to-end, but it regressed both conf_depth and reg_depth on the tighter gate versus the stable lead.

A softer continuous-rule sweep then recommended linear_qmin_qmax_wholefg_scale075 as the best half-strength fallback because it still covered all 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows while cutting effective reduction roughly in half.

That softer scale0.75 candidate also failed the tighter gate versus the stable lead, the whole-foreground quadratic scale0.5 candidate failed too, and the foreground-interior linear scale0.75 candidate still failed despite being slightly closer than the rejected whole-foreground scale0.75 fallback.

Supervised depth is already clipped by per-view foreground masks before point_masks and conf_depth_point_masks are built, so the exhausted whole-foreground/interior family is not a hidden whole-frame masking bug.

The current Camera_B1 quality knobs in training/loss.py now also support explicit conf_depth-mask routing, and the first whole-foreground hard confmask-drop candidate was executed end-to-end, but it regressed val_loss_conf_depth from 0.2289 to 0.3018 and val_loss_reg_depth from 0.1760 to 0.1959 versus the stable lead.

A follow-up coupled conf+reg candidate was also executed end-to-end: the train-only q>=2.75 whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.75 candidate was materially less damaging than hard confmask-drop, but it still regressed val_loss_conf_depth from 0.2288 to 0.2546 and val_loss_reg_depth from 0.1759 to 0.1805 versus the stable lead.

Two softer whole-foreground joint-depth interpolation candidates were then executed too: qlinear scale0.75 improved that boundary to 0.2458 / 0.1790, qquadratic scale0.75 improved it again to 0.2417 / 0.1783, and qquadratic scale0.875 reached 0.2344 / 0.1770, but all still remained worse than the stable lead while camera/T stayed flat.

A first whole-foreground decoupled conf/reg routing follow-up was then executed too: qquadratic wholefg with reg_target scale0.9375 plus conf_target scale0.875 still landed at 0.2378 / 0.1776 on conf_depth / reg_depth, so a plain whole-foreground branch split did not beat the stable lead either.

Local code review now also shows that under zju_min_supervised_views=1, the current rawpool recipe already concentrates depth supervision on the anchor-supervised view, so a plain anchor_view_only toggle is not expected to create meaningful new routing headroom by itself.

The smallest genuinely new next question therefore stays in the loss-routing dimension, but it should no longer be another whole-foreground scalar-softening point, another whole-foreground decoupled conf/reg near-neighbor, another plain edge-band softening point, another hard pixel-level depth_conf threshold, or a plain anchor_view_only restatement; it must change real non-wholefg selectivity beyond the already-tested whole-foreground and edge-band frontiers.

local_code_gaps.fact: The dataset now exposes a minimal source_policy hook (random, nearest_ring, uniform_ring), but it only changes behavior when available_view_count exceeds the requested num_images.

The dataset now also supports source_view_pool=geom_plus_raw, so the training batch can keep one supervised geom anchor while filling the remaining source-policy slots from the full raw camera ring on the real main cache.
The local finetune launcher now resolves a ZJU root against seq_names and geom_subdir, so default vggt_geom runs land on the real non-empty root instead of an empty fallback.
The local image loader now prefers np.fromfile + cv2.imdecode on non-ASCII Windows paths, so the new raw-source-view training path no longer floods local gates with avoidable cv2.imread warnings.
The dataset now supports merging multiple geom_subdirs under one root and deduplicating by camera name, so partial multi-view add-on caches can already enlarge the candidate view pool locally.
The composed dataset still only fixes image count and aspect ratio; there is no higher-level policy-conditioned sampler or learned source-selection objective.

ComposedDataset now forwards selection_anchor_quality_score into the training sample and normalizes missing selection_anchor_camera values to a collatable empty string, so quality-conditioned conf-target routing can consume cached anchor metadata without breaking DataLoader collation.
Batch repetition now duplicates selection_anchor_quality_score together with the tensor fields, so quality-conditioned candidates keep batch-aligned metadata under repeat_batch.

The rawpool line keeps fix_img_num=4 on the real main cache while expanding the candidate pool to 23 cameras via raw source-only views; all later local leads still use nearest_ring+geom_plus_raw rather than the older uniform-only scaffold.

A narrower conf_depth-only candidate now exists: it keeps the same nearest_ring+geom_plus_raw input path and only narrows conf_depth supervision by dropping the lowest-confidence extra supervised cached view when multiple supervised views appear.
The depth loss path now supports respect_conf_mask_in_grad_conf, so grad+conf confidence weighting can respect the narrowed conf_depth mask instead of continuing to weight removed pixels.

The depth loss path now also supports anchor_conditioned_conf_target_quality_min and anchor_conditioned_conf_target_quality_max, so quality-conditioned conf-target normalization can be bounded by cached anchor quality without changing camera, regression, or unproject routing.

The depth loss path now also supports anchor_conditioned_conf_target_quality_interp with quality_low and quality_high, so continuous per-sample quality-to-scale interpolation can be tested locally without changing camera, regression, or unproject routing.

Supervised depth is already clipped by the per-view foreground mask before point masks are built, so point_masks and conf_depth_point_masks already stay inside foreground support on supervised frames; the rejected whole-foreground candidates were not secretly exercising whole-frame depth supervision.

The current anchor-conditioned Camera_B1 conf-target knobs only rescale conf_reg_map inside the confidence term; they do not change conf_depth_point_masks or gt_depth point-mask eligibility, which is why the later loss-routing candidates were genuinely different from another whole-foreground or interior interpolation reskin.

Depth loss now also supports default-off continuous pixel-level depth_conf interpolation for both conf-target and reg-target routing, so a softer non-wholefg eligibility-shaping candidate can be approved without adding new dataset plumbing.

Depth loss now also supports anchor_conditioned_conf_mask_cameras plus quality- and region-conditioned train-only conf_depth-mask routing, so Camera_B1 quality rules can now change conf-depth eligibility directly rather than only rescaling conf_reg_map.

Depth loss now also supports anchor_conditioned_reg_target_* train-only joint depth-loss scaling, so a future candidate can test coupled conf+reg depth treatment rather than only conf-target normalization or hard conf-depth-mask drop.

The two full-length local cache variants that currently exist for CoreView_390, real vggt_geom and vggt_geom_4v_backup, were compared directly and provide the exact same 4-camera pool on sampled and common frames, so they do not create union headroom by themselves.

Camera supervision is now filtered at the frame level by actual supervised point support, instead of letting source-only raw views enter camera loss just because the first frame in the sample was valid.

A 32-sample supervision audit on the current nearest_rawpool lead shows that source-only raw views already have zero point pixels and zero camera/depth/unproject loss eligibility, so a plain 'exclude rawpool-only views from geometry supervision' candidate would be redundant on the current code path.

After the frame-level camera-mask fix, the stricter 10-train/5-val local gate on nearest_ring+geom_plus_raw established the rawpool stage lead: val loss_objective improves from 0.6343 to 0.2415, val loss_camera improves from 0.0644 to 0.0218, and val loss_T improves from 0.0475 to 0.0003, but depth confidence/regression still move in the wrong direction and the axis is not cloud-ready yet.

A same-input conf_depth attribution audit on the current nearest_rawpool lead shows the blocker is concentrated in extra_supervised views: anchor_supervised conf_depth_mean is 0.2978 while extra_supervised conf_depth_mean is 0.7299, and the extra_supervised delta versus baseline on the same input is +0.5498.

Keeping two supervised geom views while filling the remaining slots from rawpool was tested locally and rejected: it slightly improves val loss_objective, but it clearly worsens val camera, translation, rotation, focal, and unproject geometry losses versus baseline.

Choosing the supervised anchor by max cached depth_conf was also tested locally and rejected: despite the cached 4-view set having a stable low-confidence worst camera, the max_depth_conf anchor policy worsened val loss_objective, loss_camera, loss_T, loss_conf_depth, loss_reg_depth, and loss_unproject_geometry versus baseline.

Dropping the lowest-confidence extra supervised cached view when multi-supervised samples occur improves val loss_objective, loss_camera, loss_T, loss_reg_depth, and loss_unproject_geometry relative to the current nearest_rawpool lead, but still slightly worsens val loss_conf_depth, so it does not clear the stricter local promotion rule yet.
A 32-sample supervision audit on the dropworst_supervised variant shows exactly one active supervised view per sampled batch item and still zero geometry-loss eligibility for source-only views.

The confdepth_dropworst_only candidate became the intermediate local lead: versus the previous nearest_rawpool lead on the tighter gate, val loss_conf_depth improves from 0.2696 to 0.2514 and val loss_reg_depth improves from 0.1831 to 0.1799 while val loss_T stays at 0.0003 and val loss_camera only shifts from 0.0218 to 0.0219.

A same-input attribution audit on the confdepth_dropworst_only candidate shows extra_supervised conf-depth eligibility is reduced to zero while anchor conf_depth_mean improves by -0.0239 versus the previous nearest_rawpool lead.

The confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask follow-up remains the current stable local lead: versus confdepth_dropworst_only on the tighter gate, val loss_conf_depth improves from 0.2514 to 0.2289 and val loss_reg_depth improves from 0.1799 to 0.1760 while val loss_T and val loss_camera stay flat.

Even after the gradconfmask fix, the current stable lead is still not baseline-clean: on val, loss_conf_depth stays higher than baseline (0.2289 vs 0.1181) and loss_reg_depth stays higher than baseline (0.1760 vs 0.1181).

The completed attribution v3 audit on the current gradconfmask lead keeps conf_depth anchor-only on the audited 32-sample val slice and narrows the remaining positive camera-level gap to anchor Camera_B1.

The first anchor-conditioned follow-up was exercised locally with a train-only Camera_B1 whole-frame conf-target scale of 0.5 and failed the tighter gate: val loss_conf_depth worsened from 0.2289 to 0.3233 and val loss_reg_depth worsened from 0.1760 to 0.1901 versus the current stable gradconfmask lead.

Depth loss now also supports anchor_conditioned_conf_target_foreground_bottom_ratio, so anchor-conditioned conf-target scaling can be limited to a foreground bottom band without changing camera, regression, or unproject routing.

A narrower B1-conditioned follow-up exists locally: it keeps nearest_ring+geom_plus_raw and the confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask route, then adds a train-only Camera_B1 foreground-bottom20 conf-target scale of 0.5 as a bounded conf_depth semantics change.

The narrower Camera_B1 foreground-bottom20 conf-target scale 0.5 follow-up only produced a short-gate marginal win: on the tighter 10-train / 5-val gate, val loss_conf_depth improves from 0.2289 to 0.2287 and val loss_reg_depth improves from 0.1760 to 0.1759 versus the current stable gradconfmask lead while val loss_T and val loss_camera stay flat.

The same narrower B1-bottom20 follow-up failed the stricter 100-train / 20-val long gate: versus confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask, val loss_camera worsened from 0.0498 to 0.0499, val loss_T from 0.0209 to 0.0210, val loss_conf_depth from -0.0577 to -0.0496, and val loss_reg_depth from 0.0911 to 0.0965.

A completed quality-signal audit now sharpens the next local question: within the bad Camera_B1 anchor rows, cached quality_score correlates positively with worse delta conf_depth (r=0.6555), while non-B1 anchors remain net-improving, so a quality-conditioned conf-target semantics candidate is the next non-redundant axis.

The first bounded quality-conditioned candidate kept val loss_camera and loss_T flat but did not clear promotion: val loss_conf_depth only improves from 0.2288 to 0.2287 while val loss_reg_depth stays flat at 0.1759 versus the stable gradconfmask lead.

A postmortem on the rejected q>=3.0 candidate shows the hard threshold is over-selective: 4 / 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows still sit below quality_min=3.0, and on the first 512 train samples the rule touches only 0.7476% of conf-depth-supervised pixels.

A follow-up quality-region boundary check shows both the hard threshold and the bottom20 region are over-selective: all 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows keep positive non-bottom conf_depth deltas, while q>=3.0 + whole foreground covers 22.6975% of conf-depth-supervised pixels on a fresh 512-sample train slice versus only 0.7983% for q>=3.0 + bottom20.

A rule-shape sweep shows the next manual question should target a continuous Camera_B1 whole-foreground quality-conditioned rule rather than a broader hard threshold: q>=3.0 whole foreground still omits 4 / 10 bad audit rows, q>=2.75 still omits 1 / 10, while a continuous qmin->qmax whole-foreground reference rule covers all 10 bad rows with a 5.1155% effective reduction fraction on a fresh 512-sample train slice.

The first exact continuous Camera_B1 whole-foreground quality-conditioned candidate failed the tighter local gate: versus the stable gradconfmask lead, val loss_conf_depth regressed from 0.2289 to 0.2776 and val loss_reg_depth regressed from 0.1760 to 0.1848 even though val loss_camera only nudged from 0.0219 to 0.0218 and val loss_T stayed flat.

A softer continuous-rule sweep showed the best full-coverage fallback is linear_qmin_qmax_wholefg_scale075: it keeps all 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows active while reducing the effective train-slice conf-depth reduction fraction from 5.9433% to 2.9716% relative to the rejected exact scale0.5 rule.

The first softer continuous Camera_B1 whole-foreground candidate still failed the tighter local gate: versus the stable gradconfmask lead, val loss_conf_depth regressed from 0.2289 to 0.2483 and val loss_reg_depth regressed from 0.1760 to 0.1797 even though val loss_camera only nudged from 0.0219 to 0.0218 and val loss_T stayed flat.

The whole-foreground quadratic scale0.5 candidate also failed the tighter gate: versus the stable lead, val_loss_conf_depth regressed from 0.2288 to 0.2587 and val_loss_reg_depth regressed from 0.1759 to 0.1817 while camera/T stayed flat.

The foreground-interior linear scale0.75 candidate was slightly closer than the rejected whole-foreground scale0.75 fallback, but it still failed the tighter gate: versus the stable lead, val_loss_conf_depth regressed from 0.2288 to 0.2432 and val_loss_reg_depth regressed from 0.1759 to 0.1787 while camera/T stayed flat.

The first whole-foreground hard confmask-drop routing candidate also failed the tighter gate: versus the stable lead, val_loss_conf_depth regressed from 0.2289 to 0.3018 and val_loss_reg_depth regressed from 0.1760 to 0.1959 while val_loss_camera only nudged from 0.0219 to 0.0218 and val_loss_T stayed flat.

A conf_depth active-view-mean aggregation candidate was tested locally and is effectively identical to the current gradconfmask lead on the tighter gate: val loss_camera and loss_T stay flat, val loss_conf_depth stays at 0.2289, and val loss_reg_depth only shifts from 0.1760 to 0.1759, so this axis did not create a new promotable lead.

A new train-only rawpool exposure control was also tested locally on top of confdepth_dropworst_only: source_view_pool_train_probability=0.5 reduced avg source-only exposure from 2.75 to 1.53 views per train sample, but it sharply worsened val loss_camera, loss_T, loss_conf_depth, loss_reg_depth, loss_unproject_geometry, and objective versus the lead at that stage.

candidate_implementation_requirements: Keep the intended 4-image recipe on the real main cache while using source_view_pool=geom_plus_raw so the source-policy hook has actual headroom without depending on a full-length add-on cache.
Prefer nearest_ring+geom_plus_raw with the confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask route as the current stable local lead; the tested min_supervised_views=2 variant did not help.
Do not switch the supervised anchor to max_depth_conf as a default training rule; that variant was tested locally and rejected.

Do not open a new candidate whose only change is 'exclude rawpool-only views from geometry supervision'; that exclusion is already true on the current code path and is now backed by a local supervision audit.
Keep the respect_conf_mask_in_grad_conf fix; without it, the grad+conf branch keeps confidence-weighting pixels that the conf_depth-only routing already removed.

Do not reopen an anchor-only conf_depth target candidate under the current 4-image recipe; the audited gradconfmask lead already has zero extra_supervised and zero source_only conf-valid pixels on the 32-sample val slice.

Do not auto-open another fixed-scale Camera_B1 conf target normalization sibling; the broader whole-frame scale=0.5 candidate already failed, and the narrower bottom20 variant later failed the 100-train / 20-val long gate.

Any future candidate from this state must still be quality-conditioned rather than another constant camera-only scale: use the now-exposed selection_anchor_quality_score or an equivalent cached depth_conf signal to modulate conf-target semantics on the bad anchor.

Do not auto-open a sibling after the first quality-conditioned Camera_B1 + quality>=3.0 + foreground-bottom20 + scale 0.5 candidate failed the tighter gate and its postmortem showed the hard threshold was over-selective.

If a fresh manual question approves another quality-conditioned rule, it must justify an even softer or reshaped treatment rather than reusing the same hard q>=3.0 gate, and it still has to improve both conf_depth and reg_depth.

Do not reopen bottom20 as the implicit default region for the next question: the boundary check shows all 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows still have positive non-bottom conf_depth deltas, and q>=3.0 + whole foreground covers 22.6975% of conf-depth-supervised pixels on a fresh 512-sample train slice versus only 0.7983% for q>=3.0 + bottom20.

Do not reinterpret the next question as another broader hard-threshold whole-foreground sibling: the rule-shape sweep shows q>=3.0 whole foreground still omits 4 / 10 bad Camera_B1 audit rows and q>=2.75 still omits 1 / 10, while a continuous qmin->qmax whole-foreground reference rule covers all 10 bad rows.

Do not reopen the exact linear qmin->qmax whole-foreground scale0.5 rule: training/loss.py now exposes anchor_conditioned_conf_target_quality_interp='linear' with quality_low/high, and the first candidate on that exact shape already regressed both conf_depth and reg_depth versus the stable lead.

Do not reopen the first softer linear qmin->qmax whole-foreground scale0.75 rule either: despite halving the effective reduction fraction in the local sweep, it still regressed both conf_depth and reg_depth versus the stable lead on the tighter gate.
Do not reopen the whole-foreground quadratic scale0.5 variant either: it also regressed both conf_depth and reg_depth versus the stable lead on the tighter gate.

Do not reopen the foreground-interior linear scale0.75 erode5 variant either: it was slightly closer than the rejected whole-foreground scale0.75 fallback but still regressed both conf_depth and reg_depth versus the stable lead.

Do not spend the next candidate on a hidden whole-frame masking theory: supervised point_masks and conf_depth_point_masks are already built from foreground-clipped depth maps in training/data/datasets/zju_vggt_geom.py.

Any future manual candidate from this state must change a different dimension than region or interpolation inside anchor-conditioned conf-target normalization; the smallest locally grounded next question is to change conf_depth mask or depth-loss routing semantics in training/loss.py rather than another whole-foreground or interior reskin.

Do not reopen the first whole-foreground hard confmask-drop candidate either: the q>=2.75 whole-foreground conf_depth-mask drop variant regressed val_loss_conf_depth from 0.2289 to 0.3018 and val_loss_reg_depth from 0.1760 to 0.1959 versus the stable lead.

Do not reopen the exact q>=2.75 whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.75 candidate either: it reduced the damage versus hard confmask-drop but still regressed val_loss_conf_depth from 0.2288 to 0.2546 and val_loss_reg_depth from 0.1759 to 0.1805 versus the stable lead.

Do not reopen the qlinear whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.75 candidate either: it moved closer but still regressed val_loss_conf_depth from 0.2288 to 0.2458 and val_loss_reg_depth from 0.1759 to 0.1790 versus the stable lead.

Do not reopen the qquadratic whole-foreground joint depth-loss scale0.75 or scale0.875 candidates either: they were the closest whole-foreground routing variants so far but still regressed val_loss_conf_depth / val_loss_reg_depth to 0.2417 / 0.1783 and 0.2344 / 0.1770 respectively versus the stable lead.

If another manual candidate is approved inside the loss-routing dimension, it should change real non-wholefg selectivity rather than add another whole-foreground scalar softening point, another whole-foreground decoupled conf/reg near-neighbor, another plain edge-band scalar, another hard pixel-level depth_conf threshold, or a plain anchor_view_only toggle; prefer interpolated eligibility shaping first now that continuous pixel-level depth_conf interpolation exists locally, and keep partial joint-depth routing as the secondary allowed family.

The older threshold/pow2 geometry-gate line now provides extra negative evidence too: hard thresholding and sharper power-style weighting already failed there, which reinforces starting the next approved non-wholefg family from a smooth interpolated taper rather than another abrupt or sharpened weighting rule.

The first approved interpolated-eligibility candidate is already prebuilt as a config-only follow-up at training/config/zju_vggt_geom_unproject_source_policy_nearest_rawpool_confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask_anchorb1qquadraticfgedge5depthconfsmoothstepp60jointdepthscale0875_minimal.yaml, so approval can go directly into gate rather than spending another turn on loss.py edits.

The fastest manual approval path is now explicit too: use scripts/arm_zju_source_policy_approved_problem.py to materialize approved_problem.json from the smoothstep seed instead of hand-copying the approval contract.
That approval helper now refuses to overwrite an existing active approved_problem.json unless explicitly forced, so the single-problem contract stays fail-closed by default.

The fastest approved execution path is now explicit too: use scripts/run_zju_source_policy_research_candidate.py to run the approved config-only candidate through preflight, 1/1, 10/5, and 100/20 gating without hand-stitching the local runner commands.

That approved-candidate runner now also reuses canonical short-gate and long-gate baseline/stable reference logs from output/zju_source_policy_research_loop/gate_reference_logs.json, so a future 100/20 evaluation reruns only the candidate branch before compare/writeback.
That same runner now archives and removes the active approved_problem.json on exit, so a real run returns the research loop to IDLE_GUARD without manual cleanup.

The approved-problem contract is now explicit too: approved runs must carry first_candidate_shape and first_candidate_config, and the first interpolated approval is pinned to the prebuilt config-only smoothstep_taper candidate rather than a looser cousin.

Under the current dual-track overnight contract, any approved next question is single-candidate only: synthesize one candidate, run 1 train / 1 val, then 10 train / 5 val, and reach 100 train / 20 val only if the short gate really beats the stable lead; do not open a sibling sweep from the same approval.
Do not reopen conf_loss_aggregation=active_view_mean under the current 4-image recipe; its 10-train/5-val gate is numerically identical to the current gradconfmask lead and therefore redundant.
Treat the whole-line drop_worst_by_depth_conf_if_multi_supervised variant as a rejected branch; all later leads keep the narrower conf_depth-only route.
Do not assume lower train-time rawpool exposure is automatically stabilizing; the tested source_view_pool_train_probability=0.5 variant was locally rejected.
Do not rely on simply combining the currently available full-length caches, because the existing vggt_geom and vggt_geom_4v_backup variants are camera-identical.
Treat the full_length_addon_manifest as an alternate local data path, not as the only remaining way to unlock headroom.
Keep the frame-level camera-mask fix; without it, raw source-only views pollute the camera branch and distort the local gate.

Validate any future rawpool candidate beyond the current 10-train/5-val gate before considering cloud, because the attempted B1-bottom20 promotion already failed the 100-train / 20-val long gate and the stable gradconfmask lead still shows a large conf_depth/reg_depth gap to baseline.
Keep the current inference-side v9 override manifest as frozen local evidence, not as the new training mechanism.
non_goals: Do not reopen global zju_min_depth_conf sweeps.
Do not reopen reliable-region or bottom-only auxiliary unproject losses.
Do not restore the ghost stack.
Do not collect another manual override family by default.
required_before_cloud: Pass a stricter local gate than the 10-train/5-val probe against both baseline and confdepth_dropworst_gradconfmask under the intended 4-image recipe on the real main cache.
Do not count the current vggt_geom + vggt_geom_4v_backup pair as headroom, because their frame-aligned camera pools are identical.

Do not count the current 1-train/1-val, 3-train/1-val, or 10-train/5-val rawpool probes as full local validation; they prove the path and identify the current tradeoff, but they are still local gates rather than final evidence.
If the add-on-cache route is revisited later, do not count the current multiview add-on plan alone as headroom until the missing frames are actually materialized.
Keep cloud_gate=false until the new training question is explicitly approved.
